root_text.an5.83:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.83:1.3: Kuhako ca hoti, lapako ca, nemittiko ca, nippesiko ca, lābhena ca lābhaṁ nijigīsitā—
root_text.an5.83:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.83:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.83:2.3: Na ca kuhako hoti, na ca lapako, na ca nemittiko, na ca nippesiko, na ca lābhena lābhaṁ nijigīsitā—
root_text.an5.83:2.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.83:1.1: “Mendicants, a senior mendicant with five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.83:1.3: They use deceit, flattery, hinting, and belittling, and they use material things to chase after other material things.
translation_text.an5.83:1.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.83:2.1: A senior mendicant with five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.83:2.3: They don’t use deceit, flattery, hinting, or belittling, and they don’t use material things to chase after other material things.
translation_text.an5.83:2.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”

variant_text.an5.83:1.3: nemittiko → nimittiko (sya-all, km); nemittako (pts1ed); nimittako (mr) | nijigīsitā → nijigiṁsitā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
